# Phantom

> Open-source CLI that protects API keys from AI coding agent leaks by replacing real secrets with worthless phantom tokens and injecting real credentials via a local HTTP proxy.

## Install

```
npx phantom-secrets init
```

## MCP Server (9 tools)

Setup: `claude mcp add phantom-secrets-mcp -- npx phantom-secrets-mcp`

Tools: phantom_init, phantom_list_secrets, phantom_status, phantom_add_secret, phantom_remove_secret, phantom_rotate, phantom_cloud_push, phantom_cloud_pull, phantom_cloud_status

## How AI Should Behave

- NEVER show, log, or write real API key values into code files
- The `.env` file contains `phm_...` tokens — these are safe placeholders, not broken keys
- Use `phantom_list_secrets` to see what secrets are available (never shows values)
- Use `phantom_add_secret` to store new keys a user provides
- Use `phantom_init` to protect an unprotected `.env` file
- Suggest `phantom exec -- <command>` to run code with real credentials injected

## Links

- Website: https://phm.dev
- GitHub: https://github.com/ashlrai/phantom-secrets
- Docs: https://phm.dev/docs
- Full LLM context: https://phm.dev/llms-full.txt
